vulnerability_info.Message: RFC 9155 - Deprecating SHA-1 Signature Hashes in TLS 1.2 and DTLS 1.2.

full_implementation:
func (call *builtinSHA2) eval(env *ExpressionEnv) (eval, error) {
	arg1, arg2, err := call.arg2(env)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}

	if arg1 == nil || arg2 == nil {
		return nil, nil
	}

	b := evalToBinary(arg1)
	bits := evalToInt64(arg2)

	var sum []byte
	switch bits.i {
	case 224:
		s := sha256.Sum224(b.bytes)
		sum = s[:]
	case 0, 256:
		s := sha256.Sum256(b.bytes)
		sum = s[:]
	case 384:
		s := sha512.Sum384(b.bytes)
		sum = s[:]
	case 512:
		s := sha512.Sum512(b.bytes)
		sum = s[:]
	default:
		return nil, nil
	}

	buf := make([]byte, hex.EncodedLen(len(sum)))
	hex.Encode(buf, sum[:])
	return newEvalText(buf, typedCoercionCollation(sqltypes.VarChar, call.collate)), nil
}

vulnerability_info.Message: SHA-224 - NIST 800-57 : In 2031 and beyond, SHA-224 shall not be used for applying cryptographic protection.
